# bitchat

> On-device voice + avatar chat CLI for macOS, made by bitHuman.
> Speech-to-text + a local LLM + voice synthesis + (optional) a
> lip-synced animated face — all running locally on Apple Silicon.
> No network calls, no API keys. Source: https://www.bithuman.ai

`bitchat` is a Homebrew-installable command-line tool. A user running
it can have a real-time spoken or typed conversation with a local
assistant, optionally with a small floating avatar window that lip-
syncs. Three modes: text-only chat, audio-only voice chat, and video
chat (voice + animated face).

## Install

```
brew tap bithuman-product/bitchat
brew install bitchat
```

Requires macOS 26 (Tahoe) or later, Apple Silicon (M1+), ≥ 8 GB RAM
(16 GB recommended for video mode), ~3 GB free disk for voice and
~7 GB for video. First-launch models cache to
`~/.cache/huggingface/hub/` and
`~/Library/Application Support/bithuman/`; subsequent launches are
offline.

## Run

```
bitchat                                # voice (default) — audio chat
bitchat text                           # text-only chat
bitchat video                          # voice + animated avatar
bitchat video --image ~/me.jpg         # video chat with a portrait

# common flags (apply where relevant)
bitchat --locale ja-JP                 # Japanese ASR + TTS
bitchat --voice Aiden                  # English preset speaker (voice mode)
bitchat --voice ~/voices/me.wav        # clone a voice from a 10–20 s clip
bitchat --prompt "Be terse."           # override the system prompt
bitchat --prompt @/path/to/persona.txt # … or read from a file
bitchat --help                         # full usage
```

The bot replies after the user stops speaking. The user can:

- **Speak** to it after `🎙️ Listening`.
- **Type** a message in the launching terminal and hit Enter — same
  turn flow as a spoken utterance.
- **Cut in** by speaking while the bot is replying — barge-in cuts
  the audio and avatar within ~50 ms.

Quit with Cmd-Q (video mode), Ctrl-C, or right-click →
"Quit bitchat". All paths run a clean shutdown.

Set `BITCHAT_VERBOSE=1` to surface model-loading internals (tensor
counts, dtype breakdowns) while debugging; silent by default.

## Video mode

`bitchat video` opens a small floating circular window with a talking
face. Lip movements stay frame-paired with the bot's audio at 25 FPS.

A colored ring around the avatar signals state: cyan = listening,
violet = thinking, amber = speaking. A label below echoes the same.

Right-click the avatar to customize:

- **Choose agent…** — gallery of 8 bundled personas (face + voice +
  prompt as a coherent set). Names: Diego (default), Nova, Einstein,
  Riya, Lena, Rae, Dr. Maya, Mason. Picking a card swaps all three.
- **Change image…** — pick a portrait file or drag-drop one onto
  the avatar. ~5 s on-device encode, then the avatar speaks through
  your portrait.
- **Change voice…** — gallery of 9 voices grouped Feminine /
  Masculine. Click to audition, Save to commit.
- **Change prompt…** — multi-line editor with 6 starter templates
  (Companion, Coach, Tutor, Storyteller, Coding buddy, Calm
  listener).

After ~10 s of idle motion the avatar caches its breathing animation
and stops invoking the GPU until you speak again — quiet fans, low
battery draw.

## What's local

- **Speech-to-text** — Apple's `Speech` framework, on-device, macOS 26+.
- **Language model** — small (~2 B param) instruct LLM, 4-bit
  quantized, running through MLX.
- **Voice synthesis** — small on-device TTS (voice-cloning capable in
  voice mode).
- **Avatar animation** — bitHuman's expression engine (Wav2Vec2 →
  diffusion lip-sync → VAE).

No request leaves the machine.

## For developers

bitchat is also a Swift library, `BitChatKit`. Library access is
currently invitation-only:

```swift
import BitChatKit

var config = VoiceChatConfig()
config.localeIdentifier = "en-US"
config.systemPrompt = "You are a deadpan ship's computer. One sentence."
config.voice = .preset("Aiden")
// or .clone(referenceAudio: URL, transcript: String)

let chat = VoiceChat(config: config)
try await chat.start()
// chat.inject(userText: "hello")  — drive turns from your own UI
// AgentCatalog.all  — bundled personas
```

Open an issue at
https://github.com/bithuman-product/homebrew-bitchat/issues if you'd
like access to the source dev repo.

Apache-2.0 for the Swift source and bundled LLM/TTS weights. The
bitHuman expression-engine SDK ships under bitHuman's SDK license;
see LICENSE in the dev repo for full terms.

bitHuman Inc. — https://www.bithuman.ai. Issues / questions:
https://github.com/bithuman-product/homebrew-bitchat/issues.
